title: NOC 21210 – Mathematicians, Statisticians, and Actuaries | GTR Immigration
description: Mathematicians, Statisticians, and Actuaries play a vital role in data-driven decision-making, financial risk management, insurance modelling, research, and public-policy development across Canada. Classified under NOC 21210 (TEER 1), this occupation qualifies for skilled-immigration pathways such as Express-Entry-aligned programs, Provincial Nominee Programs (PNPs), employer-supported work permits, and long-term Permanent Residence (PR) strategies.

classification.category: 2 – Natural and applied sciences and related occupations
classification.teer: 1 – Occupations usually require a university degree
classification.major_group: 212 – Professional occupations in applied sciences (except engineering)
classification.minor_group: 2121 – Mathematicians, statisticians, actuaries, and data scientists

duties: Conduct theoretical research in algebra, geometry, probability, and logic
Apply mathematical models to engineering, computing, operations research, and management
Analyze data across scientific, business, and social-science fields
Advise on real-world applications of statistical theory
Design insurance products and calculate premiums
Provide expert testimony on financial projections
employment_requirements: Graduate degree in mathematics or statistics (for mathematicians/statisticians)
Post-secondary education in actuarial science, economics, or related field (for actuaries)
Fellowship through the Canadian Institute of Actuaries after exams + three years of work experience
job_outlook: Between 2022–2031, Canada is projected to create 4,600 new positions, with around 2,800 job seekers—indicating a strong immigration outlook, particularly for internationally trained professionals with actuarial credentials, analytics experience, and employer sponsorship.
additional_information: Demand continues to grow nationwide due to:

Expansion of insurance and financial-services sectorsData-analytics adoption in government and researchPension-fund modelling requirementsRisk-management and forecasting rolesUniversity research and consulting demand
Universities and research institutesFederal & provincial governmentsBanks and trust companiesInsurance firmsPension-consulting companiesEngineering & analytics consultancies
Annual salaries typically range between $58,500–$141,100 CAD, depending on specialization, credentials, and province.
